behavior_summary: Provides the inspect ('U8P_KEYONEC::look') name for a multi-frame key item and implements the key's 'U8P_KEYONEC::use' behavior which varies by key frame.
U8P_KEYONEC::look queries Item::getFrame(this) and displays a short name for the key variant (examples: "Key of the Caretaker", "Key of the Scion", "Key of the Necromancer").

U8P_KEYONEC::use contains the complex interactive behavior: for the Scion-frame key (frame 0x03) it prompts the player to pick a target, validates the target's type and reach, and—if valid and not already completed—drives a scripted interment/ritual sequence (animations, camera control, quake, SFX, spawned timers/helpers, creation/placement of a sprite, updating item frames) and sets a global flag to record completion.

The U8P_KEYONEC::use handler also handles common failure cases (out-of-reach barks, invalid target types) and builds/iterates a small menu/option array of candidate targets before executing the ritual path.
notable_control_flow: U8P_KEYONEC::look switches behavior by Item::getFrame(this) to select which short name to display (frames 0x00, 0x03, 0x04 mapped to different names).

U8P_KEYONEC::use branches on the key's frame to select behavior; the Scion frame follows a sequence: prompt target (suspend/process), validate type via Item::getType, check reach via Item::canReach, build/iterate local9 options, and on success spawn multiple helper processes and run the scripted sequence before setting lothInterred and updating visuals.
A guard around the ritual checks a global flag (lothInterred) to prevent re-running the sequence.
open_questions: Exact type IDs used in Item::getType checks (e.g., 0x0274/0x027D) — what concrete game object types do they represent (corpse, tomb, specific NPC body)?
Precise roles of some spawned helpers (KEYRING::func0119, FREE::func1C19, FREE::func1C8B) and their timing/coordination responsibilities in the ritual sequence.
Whether the menu built into local9 contains only corpses or also other objects, and the full conditions used to populate it (some menu-building details come from low-confidence remnants).

main_function.name: U8P_KEYONEC::look
main_function.purpose: When the player inspects the key item, display a short descriptive name corresponding to the key's current frame/variant.
entry_conditions: Branches based on Item::getFrame(this) to select which name to show.

function_map.name: U8P_KEYONEC::look
function_map.role: Display item short name for different key frames (inspect handler).

function_map.name: U8P_KEYONEC::use
function_map.role: Handle the 'U8P_KEYONEC::use' action for the key item; performs target selection/validation and runs a scripted interment/ritual sequence for the Scion-frame key (frame 0x03).

external_calls.purpose: Determine the key's frame/variant to choose the short name displayed to the player.
Check the key's frame to decide which U8P_KEYONEC::use-path to execute (notably identify the Scion-frame ritual path).
Prompt the player to select a target for the key's U8P_KEYONEC::use; the result is suspended and stored for validation.
Validate the selected target's type (compare against expected type IDs) before executing the ritual or other U8P_KEYONEC::use effects.
Check whether the avatar can reach the selected target; if false the script barks a "can't reach" message and aborts or branches accordingly.
Spawn/invoke a keyring/inventory helper on the key item early in the flow (exact purpose inferred).
Freeze player/avatar input during the scripted ritual/sequence for staging and cinematic control.
Spawn a helper to move or center the camera on the ritual's location during the sequence.
Trigger screen quake effects for dramatic emphasis during the ritual sequence.
Play sound effects at key moments in the interment/ritual sequence.
Spawned helper/timer processes likely used to schedule delays and steps in the scripted scene (inferred sequencing helpers).
Request NPC(s) to perform animations as part of the ritual cinematic.
Create/place a sprite/object at the target location as the ritual's visible result (e.g., interred Lothian sprite).
Update the frame/state of an item after the sequence to reflect its new visual or logical state.
Query positional and quantity properties of target/placement objects for camera focus and sprite placement.

reads.name: lothInterred
reads.likely_effect: Checked to determine whether the Lothian interment/ritual has already occurred; prevents re-running the sequence.

writes.name: lothInterred
writes.likely_effect: Set to record that the interment/ritual has completed, preventing repeat execution of the sequence.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides the inspect ('U8P_KEYONEC::look') name for a multi-frame key item and implements the key's 'U8P_KEYONEC::use' behavior which varies by key frame.
